title: Vascular Anomalies and Other Cutaneous Congenital Defects

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ Vascular Anomalies & Cutaneous Congenital Defects?
Infantile hemangioma (IH) พบ 4–5% ของ full-term newborns, สูงถึง 23% ใน ELBW <1,000 g — เจอที่ NICU เป็นประจำ สิ่งสำคัญ 6 ข้อ: (1) IH ≠ Kasabach-Merritt — KMP เกิดกับ KHE/tufted angioma เท่านั้น; (2) Beard distribution IH ≥4 sites → 63% risk airway hemangioma → Otolaryngology ทันที; (3) ≥5 cutaneous IH → screen hepatic IH ด้วย abdominal US + Doppler; (4) Propranolol (Hemangeol, FDA 2014) = first-line systemic IH treatment; dose 2 mg/kg/day; (5) Port-wine stain forehead/upper face → evaluate for Sturge-Weber (GNAQ mosaic mutation); (6) Giant CMN (>40 cm adult size) → melanoma risk 4.5–10% + neurocutaneous melanosis → 'feed and swaddle' MRI.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
แยก vascular tumors vs vascular malformations ด้วย ISSVA classification 2018; รู้ว่า KMP ไม่เกิดกับ IH
อธิบาย IH growth pattern (rapid proliferation 5–8 weeks, 80% complete by 3 months, 90% regress by 4 years)
ระบุ IH ที่ต้องรักษา: ulceration, disfigurement, airway, periocular, hepatic; รู้ indication แต่ละ treatment
รู้ propranolol mechanism, dose, adverse effects; แยก topical timolol indication จาก systemic propranolol
วินิจฉัย PHACE syndrome: criteria (2016), associated anomalies, workup MRI/MRA + echo + ophthalmology
แยก RICH / NICH / PICH: GLUT1 negative ทั้งหมด; รู้ว่า RICH ≠ IH แต่ can have transient coagulopathy
วินิจฉัย KHE + tufted angioma: รู้ว่านี่คือ lesions ที่ทำให้เกิด KMP จริง; sirolimus + prednisolone RCT 2022
อธิบาย port-wine stain / capillary malformation: GNAQ mutation, Sturge-Weber syndrome, laser therapy
แยก hypopigmented vs depigmented; รู้ ash leaf macule (tuberous sclerosis), café au lait (NF1 ≥6 spots)
จำแนก congenital melanocytic nevus (CMN) by size; รู้ melanoma risk, neurocutaneous melanosis, feed-and-swaddle MRI
ระบุ developmental anomalies: midline anomalies, risk stratification of spinal dysraphism markers (Box 95.2)
1. ISSVA Classification of Vascular Anomalies

การตั้งชื่อ vascular anomalies มีความสับสนมานาน เช่น 'cavernous hemangioma' ถูกใช้สำหรับทั้ง vascular tumors และ malformations ISSVA (International Society for the Study of Vascular Anomalies) classification อัพเดตล่าสุดปี 2015 → 2018 แบ่งชัดเจน 2 groups หลัก:
Vascular Tumors: cellular hyperplasia — เซลล์แบ่งตัว เจริญ แล้วถดถอย; include IH, congenital hemangioma, tufted angioma, KHE
Vascular Malformations: malformed dysplastic vessel channels — ไม่มี cellular hyperplasia, โตตามตัว, ไม่ถดถอยเอง; subdivided: capillary, venous, lymphatic, arteriovenous, combined

Key Concept: Tumor ≠ Malformation
Vascular tumors (IH, KHE) = cellular proliferation → undergo involution (some). Vascular malformations (port-wine stain, venous malformation) = structural vessel defects → grow proportionally with child, NEVER involute spontaneously. การแยกนี้ critical: ถ้า 'salmon patch' จะหาย (capillary malformation/nevus simplex) แต่ port-wine stain ไม่หาย. ห้ามเรียก port-wine stain ว่า hemangioma — ทำให้ family คาดหวังผิด.
2. Infantile Hemangiomas (IH) — Most Common Soft Tissue Tumor of Infancy
2.1 Epidemiology & Risk Factors
Incidence: 4–5% full-term newborns; up to 23% ใน preterm <1,000 g (Goelz Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2015)
Female:male ratio = 2.3–2.9:1; white predominance; risk ↑ 40% per 500 g ↓ birth weight
Additional risk factors: prematurity, multiple gestation, preeclampsia, placental abnormalities, advanced maternal age, IVF
2.2 Growth Pattern — The Classic Timeline

rows: Pale vasoconstriction halo OR telangiectatic red macule OR 'bruise-like' area
Bright red, well-demarcated; grows in volume (not radial spread)
Continued but slower growth; deep component peaks 2–3 months
Color dulls (bright red → purple → gray-white); centrifugal spread
Telangiectasias, fibrofatty tissue, skin laxity, scarring (if ulcerated)

blocks.text: Abortive IH (IH with minimal/absent growth): no proliferation phase; preferentially on lower extremities; still spontaneously regress
2.3 Clinical Types ตาม Depth
Superficial ('strawberry'): upper dermis; bright-red elevated papules/plaques with fine telangiectatic vessels in early proliferation (DDx capillary malformation)
Deep: deep dermis + subcutis; bluish dome-shaped tumor; ill-defined borders; appear ≈2–3 months; grow longer
Mixed: superficial + deep components; most common combined form
Focal vs Segmental: focal = single growth point; segmental = embryonic placode distribution → HIGHER risk of associated anomalies/syndromes
2.4 Pathogenesis — Hypoxia + CD133+ Endothelial Progenitor Cells
IH = dysregulation of vasculogenesis + angiogenesis; hypoxia likely key trigger → ↑ VEGF (via HIF-α pathway) → proliferation of CD133+ endothelial progenitor cells
Endothelial cells: GLUT1+ (glucose transporter 1) + merosin + antigen Lewis Y — same markers as placental endothelium → 'ectopic placental tissue hypothesis'
GLUT1 immunostain = specific marker for IH; not present in any other vascular tumor or malformation; congenital hemangiomas (RICH/NICH/PICH) = GLUT1 negative (North Hum Pathol 2000)
Involution: endothelial cells express caspases (apoptosis markers); blood vessels replaced by fibrofatty tissue
2.5 Diagnosis
Clinical diagnosis ใน most cases; Doppler US useful: IH = fast-flow (high Doppler signal) vs venous malformation = low-flow
MRI/angiography: large or obstructive lesions (ocular, airway); evaluate extent + associated anomalies (PHACE/LUMBAR)
Skin biopsy: if mimicking nonvascular tumor (pilomatricoma, JXG, Langerhans cell histiocytosis, rhabdomyosarcoma) → GLUT1 stain diagnostic
2.6 Complications
Local Complications

Ulceration: most common complication — up to 30% of IH; more common: lower lip, neck, intertriginous areas, diaper region (1/3 of all ulcerated IH); timing: just before rapid proliferation OR ~4 months (end of growth phase); mechanism: tissue hypoxia (tumor outgrows blood supply)

Disfigurement: nasal tip IH → 'Cyrano deformity' (alar cartilage splaying); lip IH → disrupts natural contours; breast IH in females → permanent breast hypoplasia; psychosocial sequelae: ↑ social anxiety in untreated facial IH (Costa VA JAMA Otolaryngol 2016)
Functional Complications

Periocular IH: amblyopia (direct globe pressure → astigmatism/myopia; visual axis obstruction; strabismus); large/segmental periorbital IH = highest risk; deep retrobulbar IH → proptosis; aggressive early treatment + pediatric ophthalmology evaluation
Auricular IH: cartilage destruction, physical deformity, hearing loss (sensorineural + conductive)
Potentially Life-Threatening Complications

⚠️ Airway Hemangioma — Beard Distribution = High Alert
Beard distribution: preauricular areas (L/R) + chin + lower lip + anterior neck. IH ≥4 sites in beard distribution → 63% risk symptomatic airway hemangioma (Orlow J Pediatr 1997). Facial segment 3 (mandibular) → 29% airway involvement. Presentation: biphasic stridor / hoarse croup-like cry at 6–12 weeks. Subglottis = most common site. Referral to Otolaryngology ทันที + systemic propranolol เริ่มทันที. May need multi-modal therapy (propranolol + corticosteroids + laser + surgery).

⚠️ Hepatic Hemangioma — Screen เมื่อมี ≥5 Cutaneous IH
≥5 cutaneous IH → 16% have hepatic IH vs 0% in <5 (Horii KA Pediatr Dermatol 2011). Hepatic IH → congestive heart failure + consumptive hypothyroidism. Screen: abdominal US with Doppler. Large or segmental IH ≠ ↑ risk hepatic IH. Parotid hemangioma: also associated with consumptive hypothyroidism + CHF + subglottic hemangioma.

IH ≠ Kasabach-Merritt Phenomenon
Kasabach-Merritt Phenomenon (KMP) = life-threatening thrombocytopenia + consumptive coagulopathy: ↑ D-dimer + ↓ fibrinogen + ↑ PT + ↑ PTT. KMP is caused by KHE and tufted angiomas — NOT by IH (Enjolras J Pediatr 1997). เวลา family กังวล hemorrhage จาก IH: reassure — serious bleeding rarely occurs from IH; minor trauma → short compression. ถ้าเห็น coagulopathy ร่วมกับ vascular tumor → ต้อง biopsy หา KHE/tufted angioma.
2.7 Work-Up Based on Location / Size (Table 95.3)

rows: Spinal US <3 months OR MRI spine

blocks.text: 2.8 Treatment
Active Non-Intervention (Most IH)
Reserve for: small focal IH, nonfacial, no risk of disfigurement/ulceration/functional compromise; anticipatory guidance: show before-after photos; reassure re hemorrhage risk
Up to 40% of IH leave permanent skin changes → early intervention for high-risk locations
Topical Therapy — Timolol
Timolol maleate gel 0.5% (gel-forming solution): nonselective β-blocker; most effective for superficial/thin IH; off-label; first reported 2010
Recent RCT (Munoz-Garza JAMA Dermatol 2021): timolol did NOT differ from placebo at 24 weeks for complete/near-complete resolution
Caution in preterm <44 weeks PMA + LBW <2,500 g: ↑ systemic absorption → bradycardia risk; mucosal/ulcerated sites = variable absorption
Systemic Therapy — Propranolol (First-Line)

Propranolol — FDA-Approved First-Line Systemic IH Treatment
Serendipitous discovery 2008 (Léauté-Labrèze); FDA approved Hemangeol (propranolol HCl, Pierre Fabre) in 2014. Dose: 1–4 mg/kg/day divided 2–3 times daily; practical maintenance 2 mg/kg/day; 98% response rate at mean dose 2.1 mg/kg/day. 3 RCTs: largest (Léauté-Labrèze NEJM 2015;372:735) — highest efficacy at 3 mg/kg/day × 6 months; but >2 mg/kg/day ≠ more effective + ↑ adverse events. Common AE: GI disturbance, sleep disturbance, acrocyanosis (mild, reversible). Serious AE (hypotension, hypoglycemia, bradycardia, bronchospasm): infrequent — similar frequency as placebo. Rebound growth if treated <9 months vs 12–15 months.
Corticosteroids

Prednisolone/prednisone 2–5 mg/kg/day — no longer first-line; now reserved for complex/refractory cases ± adjunct with propranolol; dramatic response 30% within 1–2 weeks; equivocal 40%; AE: cushingoid, GERD, growth retardation, hyperglycemia
Intralesional triamcinolone: small localized IH (lip, nasal tip, eyelid); periocular intralesional steroid → ophthalmic artery occlusion + blindness — only by experienced pediatric ophthalmologist
Other Therapies
Sirolimus (mTOR inhibitor): reserved for refractory IH + PHACE syndrome (Kaylani Pediatr Dermatol 2013)
Interferon alpha + vincristine: historical; NOT used now — potential AE (spastic diplegia, etc.)

Pulsed dye laser: useful during involution (residual erythema/telangiectasias); NOT useful as monotherapy for uncomplicated IH — no better than watchful waiting (Batta Lancet 2002); useful for ulceration healing; risk of ulceration during proliferative phase
Wound care for ulceration: all benefit from local wound care + occlusive dressings; petrolatum for diaper area; pain control (topical anesthetics + acetaminophen); combination therapy > monotherapy
3. Segmental Hemangioma Syndromes — PHACE & LUMBAR
3.1 PHACE Syndrome
Acronym: P=Posterior fossa malformations, H=Hemangioma, A=Arterial anomalies, C=Cardiac/Coarctation, E=Eye abnormalities; midline defects (sternal cleft, supraumbilical raphe) → sometimes 'PHACES'
Diagnostic criteria (2016 revision, Garzon J Pediatr 2016): large segmental IH >5 cm on face/scalp/neck/trunk/proximal upper extremity + 1 major OR 2 minor criteria
Incidence: female:male = 9:1; possibly more common than Sturge-Weber; ≈31% of infants with facial IH ≥22 cm² meet criteria
Segment risk: frontotemporal + frontonasal (segments 1,4) → structural cerebral + cerebrovascular anomalies; mandibular/beard (segment 3) → cardiac + airway IH; maxillary (segment 2) = lower risk
Neurologic complications: progressive cerebral artery stenosis/occlusion, moyamoya-like vasculopathy, arterial ischemic stroke

PHACE Workup — ทำทั้งหมดนี้
(1) Echocardiogram — coarctation of the aorta + other cardiac anomalies; ± cardiac MRI/MRA if abnormal. (2) MRI + MRA head and neck (with contrast) — cerebrovascular + structural anomalies. (3) Ophthalmology — retinal exam. (4) Complete physical exam — focus ventral midline defects (sternal cleft, supraumbilical raphe). (5) Additional studies as clinically indicated. Other reported comorbidities: headaches, endocrine abnormalities, hearing loss, speech delay, dysphagia.
3.2 LUMBAR / SACRAL / PELVIS Syndrome
Large lumbosacral/perineal hemangiomas → spinal dysraphism: tethered cord, lipomyelomeningocele, imperforate anus, renal anomalies, abnormal external genitalia
Prospective study: 35% of patients with lumbosacral IH >2.5 cm had spinal dysraphism on MRI (Drolet J Pediatr 2010)
Features requiring screening: large + midline + segmental + other cutaneous markers (lipoma, gluteal cleft deviation, skin tag, aplasia cutis) → MRI lumbosacral + pelvis
Segmental lumbosacral IH: often minimal/absent growth → persistent patch with coarse telangiectasias → delays in diagnosis; also high ulceration risk
4. Congenital Hemangiomas, KHE, and Tufted Angioma
4.1 Congenital Hemangiomas — RICH, NICH, PICH
Fully formed at birth; GLUT1 NEGATIVE (unlike IH); proliferation occurs in utero; usually solitary; head/neck/extremities

rows: Raised violaceous tumor ± coarse telangiectasias ± peripheral pale halo ± hypertrichosis; 3 distinct presentations
Involution begins weeks 1–few; complete by 14 months
Ulceration + bleeding shortly after birth; transient thrombocytopenia (brief, not progressive)
Blue/pink-purple plaque with coarse telangiectasias + peripheral pallor rim
Persists; grows proportionally; some become protuberant; pain during puberty
Involutes partially (until 12–30 months), then residual lesion persists like NICH
No reported complications; spectrum with RICH

blocks.text:
RICH vs IH: Key Differences
RICH = fully formed at birth, GLUT1 negative, involution by 14 months. IH = absent/minimal at birth, GLUT1 positive, rapid growth 5–8 weeks. RICH can have transient coagulopathy + thrombocytopenia (brief, not progressive — unlike KMP) → can be confused with KHE/tufted angioma → biopsy if coagulopathy + vascular tumor to confirm.
4.2 Kaposiform Hemangioendothelioma (KHE) and Tufted Angioma
KHE + tufted angioma: locally aggressive/benign spectrum; BOTH associated with life-threatening KMP
KMP mechanism: intralesional platelet trapping → profound thrombocytopenia + consumptive coagulopathy (↑ D-dimer, ↓ fibrinogen, ↑ PT, ↑ PTT)
KHE: solitary ill-defined red-purple plaque; extends viscera/chest wall/retroperitoneum; more common in neonates/infants; no cutaneous findings possible
Tufted angioma: dusky erythematous indurated plaque; overlying hypertrichosis + hyperpigmentation + telangiectasias; less aggressive infiltration; spontaneous regression in some congenital cases

KHE Treatment: Sirolimus + Prednisolone (RCT 2022)
Yi J et al. Blood 2022: RCT — sirolimus + prednisolone SUPERIOR to sirolimus monotherapy for KHE complicated by KMP. Sirolimus (mTOR inhibitor) = current standard; add prednisolone for KMP. Historical: vincristine + corticosteroids. Surgery for medically refractory.
5. Vascular Malformations
5.1 Nevus Simplex (Salmon Patch / Angel's Kiss / Stork Bite)
Very common capillary malformation; bilateral symmetric faint pink patches with feathery borders; midline predilection
Angel's kiss: forehead/eyelids — fades by 1–2 years; Stork bite: nape of neck — often persists

Lumbosacral midline nevus simplex in isolation = usually benign, NOT associated with spinal dysraphism; BUT if other cutaneous findings (lipoma, hypertrichosis, aplasia cutis, gluteal cleft deformity) → workup
Prominent/persistent nevus simplex → evaluate for Beckwith-Wiedemann, macrocephaly-CM syndrome, Nova syndrome
5.2 Capillary Malformation (Port-Wine Stain)

Incidence: 0.3% of neonates; malformed ectatic dermal capillaries; present at birth; grow proportionally; darken over time (pink → dark purple); thicken over time → papulonodular surface; pyogenic granulomas can develop within

GNAQ somatic mosaic mutation: found in both isolated CM and Sturge-Weber syndrome (SWS); in SWS also in affected brain tissue → earlier timing = more extensive neurocutaneous involvement (Shirley NEJM 2013;368:1971)

Sturge-Weber Syndrome (SWS) — Assessment ใน NICU
SWS: sporadic neurocutaneous; facial CM (upper face/forehead) + leptomeningeal vascular malformation + seizures + hemiparesis + developmental delay + choroidal malformations + glaucoma. SWS occurs in <30% of infants with facial port-wine stains — risk ↑ with hemifacial or median forehead CM. Smaller forehead CM (<half 'hemi-forehead') = very low brain involvement risk (Boos J Am Acad Dermatol 2020). Ophthalmology referral: all infants with port-wine stain involving forehead/eyelid (V1/V2) — glaucoma risk even without SWS. Laser therapy: pulsed dye laser; best results <4 years old; 20% expect 95% clearing in young age groups.
5.3 Venous Malformations
Slow-flow; ill-defined venous channels lacking smooth muscle cells; soft blue-purple tumors/plaques; ↑ size with exertion or dependent position
Localized intravascular coagulation → thrombi → calcifications (phleboliths, painful) or bleeding
Glomuvenous malformation: distinct subset; cobblestoned blue-purple plaques; often painful; GLMN gene (autosomal dominant)
Syndromes: Blue rubber bleb nevus (Bean syndrome), Maffucci (+ enchondromas), Klippel-Trenaunay
5.4 Lymphatic Malformations
Slow-flow; microcystic (<1 cm channels) vs macrocystic (cystic hygroma >1 cm); head/neck most common; life-threatening airway compromise ใน neonates
PIK3CA mutations in isolated LM + overgrowth syndromes → rapamycin as therapeutic option; surgical excision (localized), sclerotherapy, laser (superficial); cervicofacial → multidisciplinary
5.5 Arteriovenous Malformations (AVM)
Fast-flow; direct arteriovenous shunting bypassing capillaries; pulsatile + significantly warm; head/neck (including brain) most common; NEVER resolve spontaneously; progressive
RASA1 mutations → CM-AVM syndrome (multiple CMs + AVM brain/spine) + Parkes Weber syndrome; family history should be obtained
High-flow complications: significant bleeding, destructive osseous changes, high-output cardiac failure
6. Disorders of Pigmentation
6.1 Hypopigmented Lesions
Distinguish: depigmentation (complete, pure white — no melanocytes: albinism, piebaldism, vitiligo) vs hypopigmentation (subtly lighter — reduced melanin, normal melanocyte number)
Fair-skinned infants: hypopigmented lesions may require Wood lamp illumination to visualize
Nevus Depigmentosus (Nevus Achromicus)
Incidence: 0.4% of newborns; well-demarcated hypopigmented patch with irregular borders; follows lines of Blaschko if segmental
Postzygotic somatic mutation → ↓ melanogenesis; sporadic; grows proportionally; usually no extracutaneous abnormalities; rare: seizures, cognitive delay, ipsilateral limb hypertrophy
Pigmentary Mosaicism
Heterogeneous group: hypomelanosis of Ito + linear/whorled nevoid hypermelanosis + others; follow lines of Blaschko; block-like or phylloid patterns
Somatic mosaicism; CNS abnormalities (seizures, developmental delay) present by age 2 years; majority: pigmentary changes confined to skin only
Ash Leaf Macules
Small oval hypopigmented macules = classic marker for tuberous sclerosis complex; TSC = autosomal dominant; tumors in skin, CNS, kidney
6.2 Hyperpigmented Lesions
Café au Lait Macules (CALMs)
Well-demarcated oval/round light brown macules; common at birth or infancy; prevalence: 2.7% general; 2% white; 12% African-American infants

≥6 CALMs ≥0.5 cm (prepubertal) or ≥1.5 cm (postpubertal) → alert for NF1; CALMs alone ≠ fulfill NF1 criteria; associated syndromes: NF1, NF2, Legius, Noonan, LEOPARD, McCune-Albright, tuberous sclerosis, Turner (Box 95.1)
Congenital Dermal Melanocytosis (Mongolian Spots)
>90% African Americans, 81% Asians, 10% whites; blue-gray macule lumbosacral most common; fade first few years; spindle-shaped melanocytes in deep dermis; malignant change NEVER reported
Extensive lesions: can be mistakenly attributed to abuse — document carefully
Nevus of Ota / Ito
Nevus of Ota: unilateral blue/gray; orbital + zygomatic areas; ophthalmic/zygomatic branches trigeminal nerve (including sclera + fundus)

Highest frequency in Asians (up to 1% Japan); female predominance; detected at birth 60%; glaucoma 10%; rare association with malignant melanoma/uveal melanoma → ophthalmologic evaluation for periorbital involvement
Treatment: Q-switched laser devices (ruby, alexandrite, Nd:YAG)
7. Congenital Melanocytic Nevi (CMN)
CMN = hamartomas derived from neural crest cells; form in utero; present at birth or within first year. Classified by adult projected size.
7.1 Classification by Size (Adult Projected Size)
Conversion factors for adult size prediction: head × 1.7; lower extremity × 3.3; trunk/upper extremity/feet × 2.8
7.2 Neurocutaneous Melanosis (NCM)
NCM = CNS proliferation of melanocytes; associated with large/giant CMN; risk ↑ with >20 satellite lesions → 5× greater risk of NCM
Symptomatic NCM: ↑ intracranial pressure + CNS melanoma → very poor prognosis; presents within first few years of birth
MRI: T1 ↑ signal in cerebellum or anterior temporal lobes = most common finding; ~50% of asymptomatic infants with giant CMN have NCM on MRI; asymptomatic NCM alone ≠ poorer prognosis necessarily

'Feed and Swaddle' MRI ใน Giant CMN
Neonatal care providers: consider 'feed and swaddle' MRI upon recognition of NCM risk (giant CMN ± satellite lesions) — timing in neonatal period potentially avoids later general anesthesia exposure (Templeton Acta Anaesthesiol Scand 2020). CMN syndrome term: broadens NCM scope to all extracutaneous manifestations including meninges, lymph nodes, placental villi.
7.3 Management

Routine prophylactic excision generally NOT recommended: may not eliminate melanoma risk (melanocytes deep in fascia); multiple procedures; significant morbidity + can be more disfiguring than birthmark
Monitor ABCDEs: note that large CMN normally have irregular surface from birth (variably hairy, verrucous, nodular) — ABCDE criteria difficult to apply
8. Congenital Tumors & Developmental Anomalies
8.1 Nevus Sebaceous
Alopecic tan/yellow-salmon plaque; scalp/face most common; may be nodular at birth; flattens childhood; nodular again at puberty
Malignant degeneration (most commonly basal cell carcinoma): actual risk closer to 1% (lower than historically reported) (Cribier J Am Acad Dermatol 2000); rarely before puberty
Surgical excision: can be done anytime; balance lesion size vs anesthesia risk; full-thickness excision including subcutaneous tissue to ↓ recurrence
8.2 Juvenile Xanthogranuloma (JXG)
Benign self-healing non-Langerhans histiocytic tumor; derived from dermal dendrocytes; normolipemic; congenital in ~30%; 75% present year 1
Clinical: micronodular (<10 mm) or macronodular (>10 mm); early = erythematous papule; mature = characteristic yellow-orange; head/neck/trunk; involutes 3–6 years
2 clinically significant associations:
(1) Ocular JXG: <0.5% of skin lesion patients; most common cause of hyphema in infancy; glaucoma, uveitis, heterochromia iridis, proptosis; ophthalmology for <2 years with multiple lesions
(2) JXG + NF-1 + JMML triad: rare; JXG precedes leukemia diagnosis; routine JMML screening NOT recommended; hepatosplenomegaly/lymphadenopathy/pallor → workup
8.3 Mastocytosis
Increased tissue mast cells; Darier sign: rub lesion → diagnostic wheal; may blister/be hemorrhagic in neonates
Urticaria pigmentosa: most common form; multiple 1–3 cm papules on trunk; usually first 6 months; solitary mastocytoma: 2–6 cm; involution by school age (solitary) / puberty (UP)
KIT gene mutations in mast cell malignancies; diagnosis: biopsy (foamy histiocytes + Touton giant cells... wait no — mastocytosis: ↑ mast cells + tryptase + c-Kit stains)

Management: avoid triggers (friction, temperature extremes, aspirin, alcohol, narcotics, amphotericin, contrast); H1 blocker (hydroxyzine) + H2 blocker (ranitidine); corticosteroids for hypotension; perioperative H1/H2 blockers if GA required
8.4 Developmental Anomalies — Midline and Spinal Dysraphism
Midline cutaneous markers can indicate neural tube dysraphism at cranial or caudal midline.

Cranial midline mass: nasal area → dermoid cyst/sinus, encephalocele, glioma; scalp → aplasia cutis, dermoid, encephalocele, heterotopic brain tissue; 'hair collar sign' = ring of dark terminal hair surrounding a midline skin lesion → marker for ectopic neural tissue
NO biopsy without imaging first — rule out intracranial connection (CT or MRI); neurosurgery referral if intracranial connection possible

Risk Stratification — Spinal Dysraphism Markers (Box 95.2, Sewell Pediatr Dermatol 2015)
HIGH RISK: 2+ cutaneous stigmata; lipoma; acrochordon/pseudotail/true tail; aplasia cutis + congenital scars; dermoid cyst/sinus; IH ≥2.5 cm. INTERMEDIATE RISK: atypical dimple >5 mm or >2.5 cm from anal verge; IH <2.5 cm; hypertrichosis (faun tail). LOW RISK: hyperpigmentation/hypopigmentation; melanocytic nevi; simple dimple ≤5 mm in diameter ≤2.5 cm from anal verge; teratomas; port-wine stain. Imaging: MRI preferred; US acceptable <3–6 months old (before vertebral ossification) if US normal/inconclusive → MRI.
8.5 Other Developmental Anomalies

Preauricular pits/sinuses: common; bilateral 25–50%; associated syndromes (Branchiootorenal, Goldenhar, Waardenburg); renal US only if dimorphic features + family history deafness + maternal GDM + auricular/renal malformation

Accessory tragus: pedunculated papule from preauricular area to angle of mouth (embryonic fusion line 1st branchial arch); associated: Goldenhar, Treacher Collins, VACTERL; suture ligation CONTRAINDICATED — can contain cartilage contiguous with external canal; surgical excision by experienced surgeon only
Supernumerary digits: contain cartilage + nerves + nail; suture ligation → traumatic neuroma + skin necrosis → surgical excision with nerve dissection
Supernumerary nipples (polythelia): along embryologic milk lines; usually no workup unless glandular tissue → complete surgical excision (enlarges at puberty)
9. NICU Practical — Vascular Lesion / Pigmented Lesion ใน Ward
IH ที่เจอแรกเกิด: แยกจาก capillary malformation ด้วย fine telangiectatic vessels arising in proliferation phase; IH = GLUT1 positive; ใน ELBW neonate: incidence สูง 23% — คาดว่าจะเจอ IH บ่อยใน NICU
Beard distribution IH: นับ sites; ≥4 sites → 63% airway IH → ส่ง ENT + เริ่ม propranolol; monitor stridor/hoarse cry at 6–12 weeks

เริ่ม propranolol: confirm no contraindication (AV block, decompensated CHF, reactive airway, hypoglycemia risk); start 1–2 mg/kg/day, uptitrate to 2 mg/kg/day divided 2–3×/day; monitor HR, BP, glucose first doses; treat minimum 12–15 months
Port-wine stain forehead/eyelid: always refer ophthalmology (glaucoma risk); if extensive hemifacial/forehead → SWS workup; start pulsed dye laser early (<4 years) for best results

Giant CMN (>40 cm adult projection): count satellite nevi; ≥20 satellites → 5× ↑ NCM risk → 'feed and swaddle' MRI นัดหมาย; dermatology consult; educate parents re ABCDE monitoring; routine excision ไม่แนะนำ
Café au lait ≥6 ขนาด ≥0.5 cm: alert for NF1; CALMs alone ≠ diagnostic; look for other NF1 features; genetics referral
Ash leaf macule (Wood lamp): screen for tuberous sclerosis — brain US/MRI, cardiac echo (rhabdomyoma), renal US
Mastocytosis ใน neonate: avoid triggers; Darier sign (dermographism/wheal after rubbing) = diagnostic; H1 blocker; if GA needed → perioperative H1/H2 pretreatment
Midline dimple: apply risk stratification (Box 95.2); simple dimple ≤5 mm ≤2.5 cm from anal verge = low risk → no imaging required; high-risk features → MRI
Accessory tragus/supernumerary digit: ห้าม suture ligation ทั้งคู่ — traumatic neuroma risk; refer surgical team for proper excision
10. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — Vascular Tumor ≠ Vascular Malformation: KMP เกิดกับ KHE เท่านั้น
IH ไม่เคยทำให้เกิด Kasabach-Merritt phenomenon. ถ้าเห็น coagulopathy (↑ D-dimer + ↓ fibrinogen + ↑ PT/PTT + thrombocytopenia) ร่วมกับ vascular tumor → บอก family: 'nี่ไม่ใช่ IH' → suspect KHE/tufted angioma → biopsy ยืนยัน. Treatment: sirolimus + prednisolone (Yi Blood 2022). RICH สามารถมี transient mild thrombocytopenia ได้ แต่ไม่ progressive.

PEARL 2 — Beard Distribution + Stridor = Airway Hemangioma จนกว่าจะพิสูจน์ไม่ใช่
Beard distribution: preauricular + chin + lower lip + anterior neck — ≥4 sites → 63% risk airway IH. Biphasic stridor / hoarse croup-like cry อายุ 6–12 สัปดาห์ = classic presentation. Subglottis = most common site. Action: ENT evaluation ทันที + systemic propranolol เริ่มทันที. Facial segment 3 alone → 29% risk. ห้าม discharge infant ที่มี beard distribution IH ≥4 sites โดยไม่ evaluate airway.

PEARL 3 — Propranolol = First-Line IH; 2 mg/kg/day × 12–15 Months
Propranolol (Hemangeol, FDA 2014): 2 mg/kg/day divided BID-TID; treat 12–15 months (ไม่ใช่แค่ 6 months) เพื่อ prevent rebound growth. Timolol 0.5% gel: ใช้แค่ thin/superficial IH; recent RCT ≠ placebo at 24 weeks for complete resolution. Avoid propranolol: AV block, decompensated CHF, reactive airway disease. Corticosteroids: reserve for complex/refractory + as adjunct — no longer first-line.

PEARL 4 — Port-Wine Stain + Forehead/Eyelid = Always Refer Ophthalmology (Glaucoma Risk)
Glaucoma risk ใน port-wine stain ≠ ต้องมี Sturge-Weber. ทุก infant ที่ port-wine stain ถูก forehead + upper/lower eyelid (V1/V2 trigeminal pattern) → complete ophthalmologic exam. SWS pattern: hemifacial + median forehead CM → higher brain involvement risk (GNAQ mosaic mutation). Smaller forehead CM (<half hemi-forehead) = very low brain risk (Boos 2020). Laser therapy: start early <4 years — younger age → better clearing.

PEARL 5 — Giant CMN + ≥20 Satellites = Feed-and-Swaddle MRI สำหรับ NCM
Giant CMN (>40 cm adult size): melanoma risk 4.5–10%, 70% before age 10. ≥20 satellite nevi → 5× ↑ risk of neurocutaneous melanosis (NCM). NCM: symptomatic → ↑ ICP + CNS melanoma → very poor prognosis. Opportunity in NICU: 'feed and swaddle' MRI — avoids general anesthesia later (Templeton 2020). Routine excision NOT recommended — ไม่ eliminate melanoma risk (melanocytes deep in fascia).
11. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies

Mulliken JB, Glowacki J. Plast Reconstr Surg 1982;69:421: Original classification of vascular anomalies into hemangiomas (endothelial hyperplasia) vs vascular malformations (structural channel defects) — foundational work that ended decades of nomenclature confusion.

Wassef M et al. Pediatrics 2015;136:e203 (ISSVA 2015): Updated ISSVA classification of vascular anomalies — current standard with 2018 expansion; divides into vascular tumors (benign/locally aggressive/malignant) and vascular malformations (simple/combined/associated with syndromes).

North PE et al. Hum Pathol 2000;31:11: GLUT1 as immunohistochemical marker for IH — highly selective; positive in IH and placental endothelium but absent in all other vascular tumors/malformations including congenital hemangiomas (RICH/NICH/PICH).

Léauté-Labrèze C et al. N Engl J Med 2015;372:735: Pivotal RCT of oral propranolol for IH (HEMANGIOL trial) — largest trial; highest efficacy at 3 mg/kg/day × 6 months; foundation for 2014 FDA approval; formed propranolol as first-line systemic therapy.
Horii KA et al. Pediatr Dermatol 2011;28:245: ≥5 cutaneous IH → 16% had hepatic IH vs 0% in <5 — established threshold for abdominal US screening.
Orlow SJ, Isakoff MS, Blei F. J Pediatr 1997;131:643: Beard distribution IH: ≥4 sites → 63% risk symptomatic airway hemangioma — established clinical screening rule now in guidelines.
Enjolras O et al. J Pediatr 1997;130:631: KMP does NOT occur with 'true' IH — clarified that KMP is associated with KHE/tufted angioma only; corrected decades of misattribution.
Yi J et al. Blood 2022: RCT: sirolimus + prednisolone superior to sirolimus monotherapy for KHE complicated by KMP — established combination as current standard of care.

Metry DW et al. Pediatrics 2009;124:1447; Garzon MC et al. J Pediatr 2016;178:24: PHACE syndrome consensus diagnostic criteria (2009) and revised care recommendations (2016) — definitive diagnostic framework including 2016 revision to include neck/upper trunk IH.
Drolet BA et al. J Pediatr 2010;157:789: Prospective study: 35% of lumbosacral IH >2.5 cm had spinal dysraphism on MRI — established MRI threshold for LUMBAR/SACRAL workup.

Shirley MD et al. N Engl J Med 2013;368:1971: GNAQ somatic mosaic mutation causes both Sturge-Weber syndrome and isolated port-wine stains — SWS results from earlier mutation timing affecting both skin and brain.
Boos MD et al. J Am Acad Dermatol 2020;83:1110: Smaller forehead CM (<half hemi-forehead) at very low risk for brain involvement in SWS — helps stratify which infants need urgent neurologic workup.

Munoz-Garza FZ et al. JAMA Dermatol 2021;157:583: RCT of topical timolol vs placebo for early proliferative phase IH — timolol did NOT differ from placebo at 24 weeks for complete/near-complete resolution; challenges widespread topical timolol use.
Marghoob AA et al. Arch Dermatol 1996;132:170: Large CMN and melanoma risk — prospective study establishing 4.5–10% melanoma risk in large/giant CMN; 70% diagnosed before age 10.

Sewell MJ, Chiu YE, Drolet BA. Pediatr Dermatol 2015;32:161: Neural tube dysraphism: review of cutaneous markers and imaging — current risk stratification framework (high/intermediate/low risk) for spinal dysraphism workup; simple dimple = low risk.
12. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: Neonate อายุ 3 สัปดาห์ มี vascular lesion บนแก้ม เพิ่งสังเกตเห็นหลังคลอด ขณะนี้เป็น bright-red elevated papule with fine telangiectatic vessels — diagnosis? บ่งบอกอะไร?

ตอบ: Diagnosis: infantile hemangioma (IH), superficial type. Fine telangiectatic vessels ใน early proliferation phase = distinguishing feature from capillary malformation. Timing: IH present minimally/absent at birth, then precursor lesion, rapid proliferation weeks 5–8. ขณะนี้อยู่ใน early rapid growth phase. ต้องประเมิน: location (facial → risk disfigurement), size, pattern (focal vs segmental). GLUT1 stain positive ถ้าต้องการ confirm; ไม่จำเป็นต้อง biopsy ถ้า clinical appearance typical.
Q: Infant อายุ 8 สัปดาห์ มี IH ที่ left cheek + left lower lip + chin + bilateral preauricular areas (5 sites) — what are you worried about? ต้องทำอะไร?

ตอบ: Beard distribution IH ≥4 sites → 63% risk symptomatic airway hemangioma. Action: (1) Referral to ENT/otolaryngology ทันทีเพื่อ laryngoscopy evaluate airway; (2) เริ่ม systemic propranolol ทันที ไม่รอ symptoms. ≥5 cutaneous IH (ที่นี่ = 5 sites) → also screen for hepatic IH: abdominal US + Doppler. Monitor for biphasic stridor/hoarse croup-like cry. Facial segment 3 (mandibular/beard) lesion → cardiac screening (echo) and airway evaluation.
Q: แยก IH จาก RICH และ port-wine stain อย่างไร? ทั้งสามอย่างเป็น vascular lesions ที่อาจเห็นแรกเกิด

ตอบ: IH: absent/minimal at birth, rapid proliferation 5–8 weeks, GLUT1 positive, involutes; RICH (Rapidly Involuting Congenital Hemangioma): fully formed at birth, GLUT1 NEGATIVE, involution by 14 months (faster than IH regression); violaceous tumor ± telangiectasias ± peripheral pale halo; can have transient thrombocytopenia (not KMP); Port-wine stain (capillary malformation): present at birth, NEVER involutes, grows proportionally, darkens over time; faint pink patch with feathery border → pinkish at birth; vascular malformation not tumor — no cellular proliferation.
Q: Infant มี large segmental IH บน right face >5 cm ครอบคลุม forehead และ periorbital area — diagnosis? workup?

ตอบ: Concern: PHACE syndrome. PHACE: Posterior fossa abnormalities + Hemangioma + Arterial anomalies + Cardiac/Coarctation + Eye abnormalities. Frontotemporal/frontonasal IH (segments 1,4) → highest correlation with cerebrovascular anomalies. Workup: (1) MRI/MRA brain + neck with contrast, (2) Echocardiogram (coarctation of aorta), (3) Ophthalmology retinal exam, (4) Complete physical exam for ventral midline defects. ≈31% of facial IH ≥22 cm² meet diagnostic criteria. Female:male = 9:1. Possible complications: arterial ischemic stroke, moyamoya-like vasculopathy.
Q: Port-wine stain involving forehead and upper eyelid — ต้องทำ workup อะไร? โรคที่เกี่ยวข้องคืออะไร?

ตอบ: Port-wine stain forehead/eyelid (V1/V2 distribution) → risk of Sturge-Weber syndrome (SWS) + glaucoma. Ophthalmologic exam ทุกราย — glaucoma risk แม้ไม่มี SWS. SWS: GNAQ somatic mosaic mutation; leptomeningeal vascular malformation → seizures + hemiparesis + developmental delay. SWS occurs in <30% of infants with facial port-wine stain; risk ↑ with hemifacial + median forehead pattern. Small forehead CM (<half hemi-forehead) = very low brain risk. Laser therapy: pulsed dye laser; start early <4 years; 20% achieve 95% clearing in young age groups.
Q: Neonate มี large blue-gray macule at lumbosacral area — diagnosis? ต้องทำ imaging หรือไม่?

ตอบ: Diagnosis: congenital dermal melanocytosis (Mongolian spot) = blue-gray macule ใน deep dermis; spindle-shaped melanocytes. Epidemiology: >90% African Americans, 81% Asians, 10% whites. Malignant change NEVER reported. Imaging NOT required for typical lumbosacral Mongolian spot in isolation. แต่ถ้ามี additional features ที่น่ากังวล (อยู่ใน midline + อื่นๆ) → ต้องแยกจาก IH/capillary malformation ซึ่งอาจต้องทำ spinal imaging. Document carefully — extensive lesions can be misattributed to abuse.
Q: Infant มี large congenital melanocytic nevus (CMN) on back ≈ 25 cm projected adult size + 25 satellite nevi — ต้องทำอะไรใน neonatal period?

ตอบ: Giant CMN (>20 cm adult size) + ≥20 satellite nevi → 5× ↑ risk of neurocutaneous melanosis (NCM). NCM: CNS proliferation of melanocytes → symptomatic = ↑ ICP + CNS melanoma → poor prognosis. 'Feed and swaddle' MRI ใน neonatal period — avoids general anesthesia later (Templeton 2020). Melanoma risk: 4.5–10%; 70% before age 10. Routine excision NOT recommended (melanocytes deep in fascia; morbidity high). Refer dermatology. Educate parents re ABCDE monitoring.
Q: Infant อายุ 1 เดือน มี lesion บริเวณ lumbosacral area — dimple ขนาด 4 mm ห่างจาก anal verge 2 cm — ต้องทำ spinal imaging หรือไม่?

ตอบ: Apply risk stratification (Sewell Pediatr Dermatol 2015): simple dimple ≤5 mm diameter + ≤2.5 cm from anal verge = LOW RISK. This dimple: 4 mm ≤5 mm, 2 cm ≤2.5 cm → LOW RISK — NO screening imaging required. Upgrade to intermediate/high risk if: >5 mm, >2.5 cm from anal verge, associated with other stigmata (lipoma, hypertrichosis, IH ≥2.5 cm, aplasia cutis, dermoid, skin tag, deviated gluteal cleft). Explain to family: isolated simple dimple does not require investigation.
Day 96 Preview

Avery Chapter 96 — Neonatal Skin Infections
พรุ่งนี้: Neonatal Skin Infections — หลังจาก Day 95 ที่เรียน vascular anomalies, pigmented lesions, congenital tumors; Day 96 จะครอบคลุม bacterial skin infections (Staphylococcal scalded skin syndrome, MRSA), fungal infections (Candida), viral infections (HSV, VZV, molluscum) ใน neonates. Clinical recognition ใน NICU, distinguishing infectious vs congenital lesions, empiric antibiotic/antiviral choices, และ infection control principles.
